You said:
I/flutter (15574): User logged out from Firebase and Google services I/FIAM.Display(15574): Unbinding from activity: MainActivity I/FIAM.Headless(15574): Removing display event component I/FIAM.Display(15574): Binding to activity: com.google.android.gms.auth.api.signin.internal.SignInHubActivity I/FIAM.Headless(15574): Setting display event component I/FIAM.Display(15574): Unbinding from activity: com.google.android.gms.auth.api.signin.internal.SignInHubActivity Doesnt work after making my app release
ChatGPT said:

It looks like your app is experiencing an issue with Firebase Authentication after being released. Here are some things to check:

1. Check Your SHA Fingerprint

  • When using Firebase Authentication (especially Google Sign-In), you need to register your app’s SHA-1 and SHA-256 keys in Firebase.

  • To get your SHA keys, run:

    sh
    keytool -list -v -keystore ~/.android/debug.keystore -alias androiddebugkey -storepass android

    (For release mode, use your actual keystore path instead of the debug one.)

  • Go to Firebase Console > Project Settings > Your App and ensure the correct SHA keys are added.

2. Enable Google Sign-In in Firebase Console

  • Make sure Google Sign-In is enabled in Firebase Console:
    • Go to Authentication > Sign-in method > Google.
    • Ensure it is enabled and the web client ID is correct.

3. Check Your google-services.json

  • Ensure you have the correct google-services.json file in the android/app/ directory.
  • If you generated the JSON file before setting up SHA, re-download it from Firebase and replace the old one.

4. Proguard Rules (for Release Mode)

  • If Proguard is enabled (minifyEnabled true in android/app/build.gradle), it might be stripping out necessary Firebase classes.

  • Add this to your proguard-rules.pro:

    kotlin
    -keepattributes *Annotation*
-keep class com.google.android.gms.** { *; }
-keep class com.google.firebase.** { *; }
-keep class com.google.auth.** { *; }
-keep class android.support.v4.** { *; }

5. Internet Permissions

  • Ensure AndroidManifest.xml has internet permissions:
    xml
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET"/>

6. Check Release Logs

  • Run your release APK in a real device and check logs with:
    sh
    flutter logs

If it still doesn’t work, let me know what happens when you try to log in! 🚀

You said:
How to turn app back into debug
ChatGPT said:

To run your Flutter app in debug mode again, follow these steps:

1. Run the App in Debug Mode

The simplest way to run your app in debug mode is to use the command:

sh
flutter run

This will automatically run the app in debug mode.

If you are using a physical device or emulator, specify it:

sh
flutter run --debug

2. Change Build Mode in Android Studio

If you are using Android Studio:

  1. Open Run > Edit Configurations.
  2. Select your app configuration.
  3. Make sure the Build Variant is set to debug.
  4. Click Apply and OK.

3. Revert Release Config in AndroidManifest.xml

If you modified AndroidManifest.xml for release, make sure debuggable is enabled:

xml
<application
    android:debuggable="true"
    ...>
</application>

However, this is usually controlled by the build type.

4. Switch to Debug Mode in build.gradle

Make sure your android/app/build.gradle has:

gradle
buildTypes {
    debug {
        debuggable true
    }
    release {
        debuggable false
    }
}

If you previously built a release version, run:

sh
flutter clean

to remove cached build files.

5. Install Debug APK

To explicitly install a debug APK:

sh
flutter build apk --debug
adb install build/app/outputs/flutter-apk/app-debug.apk

Let me know if you need more help! 🚀
